interactions.summary: Jiang rejects the standard classroom explanation for Allied intervention in Russia and says states served private capital, not national interest or ideology. A Richard Spence reading is introduced to argue Wall Street promoted the Bolsheviks as a business opportunity despite public claims about democracy and order.
Student reads quoted source material that Jiang immediately interprets in the surrounding focus refs.

speaker_notes.note: Main speaker is Jiang; diarization labels are treated as hints, while student readings/questions are separated when context shows them.

claims.claim: Jiang says Allied armies entered Russia not primarily to fight communism but to force Bolsheviks to pay debts owed to Britain, Japan, the United States, and France.
claims.temporal_scope: Claim stated in the December 16, 2025 lecture.

claims.claim: Anglo-American foreign policy is framed as serving private transnational capital rather than national interest.
claims.temporal_scope: Claim stated in the December 16, 2025 lecture.

claims.claim: Transnational capital is said to care about profit rather than ideology, which is why it could work with Bolsheviks despite anti-capitalist rhetoric.
claims.temporal_scope: Claim stated in the December 16, 2025 lecture.

claims.claim: Richard Spence's account is used to argue Wall Street had pro-Bolshevik pitchmen who publicly described Bolshevik Russia as a democratic and excellent business environment.
claims.temporal_scope: Claim stated in the December 16, 2025 lecture.

signature_moments.moment: Foreign policy is not the state pursuing its interest; it is private capital wearing the state as a glove.
signature_moments.why_it_matters: This is one of the episode's clearest empire definitions.

signature_moments.moment: Revolution is attractive to capital because everything goes on sale.
signature_moments.source_phrase: revolution is an opportunity to steal as much as you can
signature_moments.why_it_matters: It links ideological upheaval to asset-stripping.

glossary_terms.term: Wall Street and the Russian Revolution
glossary_terms.definition_or_usage: Richard Spence book Jiang uses as documentary support for Wall Street support of Bolsheviks.
Jiang's explanation for why Bolsheviks would take capitalist help temporarily.

chronology_notes.note: Dated December 16, 2025, this source follows the Secret History sequence and sets up the next class on Pax Judaica.

uncertainty_notes.note: Student reading from Spence is quoted material; Jiang's interpretation appears in surrounding segments.
